title: OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to give Codex persistent cloud workspaces
friendlyTitle: OpenAI wants Codex agents that can keep working in the cloud
summary: OpenAI said it will acquire Ona, a company focused on secure cloud execution and orchestration, to expand Codex with persistent, customer-controlled environments. OpenAI says more than 5 million people use Codex weekly and that usage is up 400% from earlier in 2026.
friendlySummary: OpenAI plans to buy Ona so Codex can run in secure cloud environments over longer periods. The idea is for agents to keep working even after a local session ends.
whyItMatters: Coding agents are moving from short local sessions toward longer-running delegated work. Persistent, governed environments could make agents more useful for tests, migrations, bug fixing, and security work while preserving enterprise control.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make coding agents more like supervised teammates for long tasks. The key benefit is continuity; the key risk to manage is access and review.
